exercises.title: Gate Set Compiler
exercises.description: Build a compiler that decomposes circuits into fault-tolerant primitives.
tasks: Input: circuit with H, CNOT, R_z(θ) gates
Decompose R_z rotations into Clifford + T
Assign implementation method to each gate
Output: sequence of FT operations
Count total T gates (T-count)

exercises.title: T-Count Optimizer
tasks: Identify T-T cancellations (T·T = S)
Find commuting T gates for merging
Apply template-based optimizations
Compare T-count before and after
Report optimization ratio
hints: T commutes with Z, not with X or H
Look for patterns like H·T·H sequences

exercises.title: Resource Estimator
exercises.description: Estimate full resources for a fault-tolerant algorithm.
tasks: Input: algorithm parameters (T-count, logical qubits)
Calculate required code distance for target error
Size magic state factory for given consumption rate
Estimate total physical qubits
Estimate total execution time

exercises.title: Full-Stack Simulator
tasks: Compile high-level circuit to FT primitives
Simulate logical operations on encoded qubits
Include QEC cycles between operations
Inject T gates from simulated factory
Track Pauli frame and final measurement
